verificationScope: Repository evidence and GitHub Actions on draft pull request #10. Several modules exist independently, but a complete integrated cycle from product objective through sandboxed implementation, release, measured outcome and learning is not yet claimed.

capabilities.summary: The Next.js workspace and shared product packages provide the current human-facing foundation for product setup, auth, data, security, integrations and future lifecycle control surfaces.
limitations: Research, execution, verification, release and learning are not yet exposed through one unified workspace
Current app features are a foundation rather than the final Shipkit lifecycle interface

capabilities.summary: The generator creates a product workspace with independent identity, application foundation and AI workflow while retaining connection to Shipkit tooling.
checks: AI workflow check: generated project integration

capabilities.summary: Deterministic evidence-gated lifecycle stages govern the unified product loop with rejection, rollback and inconclusive outcomes.
A0-A4 autonomy and R0-R4 risk policy use cycle-bound exact-scope, versioned, expiring and revocable approvals for protected product actions.
checks: Unit tests cover wrong scope and expired approval rejection

capabilities.summary: Append-only synchronized journals, atomic snapshots, corruption recovery, checksums and serialized writers preserve the shared Shipkit lifecycle.
checks: Snapshot recovery and interrupted trailing-write tests
limitations: Event-schema migration fixtures are not complete
Kill-process tests at every persistence boundary are not complete
A multi-process stress suite is not complete

capabilities.summary: Immutable SHA-256 blobs are separated from contextual occurrences so research, execution, verification, release and outcome claims can share trustworthy evidence.
checks: Distinct occurrence records for identical blobs

capabilities.summary: Shipkit currently models repository structure, checks, CI, product signals, Git state and structural trust boundaries; deeper user and operational understanding remains planned.
limitations: User journeys, analytics, support, deployment state and product outcomes are not yet integrated

capabilities.summary: Discovered package scripts run without a shell in temporary source copies with timeout, bounded output, reduced environment and secret redaction, feeding the shared readiness model.
limitations: Temporary workspace is not a security sandbox
Filesystem write containment is not enforced

capabilities.summary: Modern internal, external and product research will create reproducible sources, atomic claims, contradictions, opportunities and experiments inside the same product cycle.
checks: No complete research-to-decision product workflow yet

capabilities.summary: Capability-negotiated sandboxes and interchangeable command or coding-agent adapters will implement approved experiments without owning state or acceptance.
checks: No real untrusted sandbox or agent-delivery integration yet

capabilities.summary: Independent verification, provenance, explicit release approval, deployment adapters and rollback will extend the same Shipkit cycle into operations.
checks: No complete authorized release and rollback cycle yet

capabilities.summary: Technical and product outcome measurement will drive keep, iterate, rollback and causally evaluated memory or skill improvement in later cycles.
checks: No measured end-to-end learning cycle yet
